panels.description: Medium shot on the deck of the Hispaniola. Israel Hands sits, his face a mask of haggard, twisted malice as he watches Jim Hawkins. The lighting is harsh sunlight reflecting off the wooden deck, creating deep, treacherous shadows on Hands' face.

bubbles.text: His eyes followed me derisively, a shadow of treachery in every line of his face.

panels.description: Wide shot of the ship at sea. The sails are adjusted, the horizon is wide and lonely. Jim and Hands sit on the deck near a simple meal. Silence hangs between them, oppressive and heavy.
Close-up on Hands, gesturing toward the body of O'Brien slumped nearby. Hands looks sickly, his expression conveying false worry about the 'unlucky' ship.

bubbles.text: Cap'n, here's my old shipmate, O'Brien; s'pose you was to heave him overboard? He ain't ornamental now.

panels.description: Extreme close-up on Hands' eyes—they are shifty, constantly glancing at the sky and then toward the cabin door. His tongue pokes out nervously.

bubbles.text: Step down into the cabin and get me some wine, Jim. This brandy's too strong for me.

panels.description: Medium shot: Jim Hawkins standing, looking down at Hands with a sharp, guarded expression. Hands looks up, feigning ignorance while hiding his true intent.

bubbles.text: Some wine? Far better. Will you have white or red?
Don't matter, shipmate. Just get it.
